meta.name: SWEMWBS Short Warwick-Edinburgh Mental Well-Being Scale
meta.description: The SWEMWBS is a 7-item scale derived from the full 14-item WEMWBS, measuring mental well-being with a focus on functioning rather than feeling. It provides a single score reflecting overall mental well-being over the past two weeks.
meta.author: Stewart-Brown, Tennant, Tennant, Platt, Parkinson & Weich (2009)
meta.reference: Stewart-Brown S, Tennant A, Tennant R, Platt S, Parkinson J, Weich S. Internal construct validity of the Warwick-Edinburgh Mental Well-Being Scale (WEMWBS): a Rasch analysis using data from the Scottish Health Education Population Survey. Health Qual Life Outcomes. 2009;7:15.
meta.instruction: Below are some statements about feelings and thoughts. Please select the option that best describes your experience of each over the last 2 weeks.

questions.text: I've been feeling optimistic about the future
I've been dealing with problems well
I've been feeling close to other people
I've been able to make up my own mind about things

ranges.label: Low Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being score is low, suggesting you may be experiencing significant difficulties in your day-to-day functioning and emotional health. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Consider seeking support from a mental health professional
Talk to a trusted friend, family member, or support service
Try to establish small daily routines that bring structure
Be kind to yourself; recovery takes time
Try doing one fulfilling activity each day

ranges.label: Below Average Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is below average. You may be finding some aspects of daily life challenging. Your well-being is at a moderate level. Increasing positive experiences and social connections may help improve your overall sense of fulfillment.
suggestions: Identify activities that give you a sense of accomplishment or enjoyment
Stay connected with others and maintain social relationships
Consider mindfulness or relaxation practices
If you are struggling, do not hesitate to seek professional support
Increase the frequency of positive social activities

ranges.label: Above Average Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is above average. You generally feel positive about your functioning and relationships. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Continue engaging in activities that support your well-being
Maintain your social connections
Practice gratitude and positive thinking
Stay physically active to maintain good mental health
Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle

ranges.label: High Well-Being
ranges.description: Your mental well-being is high. You feel optimistic, capable, and connected to others. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Keep up the positive habits that support your well-being
Consider helping others to further enhance your sense of purpose
Continue to nurture your relationships and personal growth
Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
